title: No ceremony seeds the agents tier the jit loader resolves from — a fresh substrate cannot produce a resolvable target_agent from the loop's own operating context
canonical_vocabulary: agents-tier seeding in the install ceremony
description: The jit agent loader's project tier is <contextDir>/agents/ (pi-jit-agents agent-spec.ts:219-243; ~/.pi/agent/agents/ second; builtinDir third only when the caller supplies one). The work-order loop and call-agent construct the loader with cwd only (work-order-loop.ts:116, call-agent-tool.ts:73) — no builtinDir — so the 30+ packaged specs in pi-workflows/agents/ and pi-behavior-monitors/agents/ (resolved by those packages' own loaders) are unreachable for work-order dispatch. Nothing creates or seeds <contextDir>/agents/: context-init/accept-all/install materialize config, schemas, and starter blocks only; no agents concept exists in the install ceremony or samples catalog. The only in-band materializer is author-agent-spec (mkdirs on first authoring, author-agent-spec-tool.ts:82-83) — which is interactively gated (FGAP-126), so on a fresh substrate driven from pi -p the loop's precondition is unproducible. Class: the install ceremony seeds a subset of the surfaces the runtime resolves from (schemas and blocks seeded; agents not; templates similar but package-defaulted).

evidence.reference: 219-243 three-tier search; docblock's '.project/agents' wording stale (resolves via tryResolveContextDir)
createAgentLoader({ cwd }) constructed with no builtinDir (call-agent-tool.ts same) — the packaged specs are off this tier's search path
Break 4: ceremony survey (install copies installed_schemas/installed_blocks only), AgentNotFoundError reproduction, compounding with the gated author tool

impact: run-work-order-loop on a fresh substrate throws AgentNotFoundError for every target_agent; combined with FGAP-126, the loop's precondition cannot be produced in the loop's own operating context — hand-writing spec files into an unseeded directory is the only path.
proposed_resolution: Under FEAT-014, per the dispatch-architecture decision: seed the agents tier in the install ceremony (an installed_agents[] manifest beside schemas/blocks) and/or wire a bundled builtinDir into the dispatch loaders; align the loader docblock's stale tier wording.
